extracted_codes.context: Patient lab test: serum creatinine - referenced in Table 1 and AKIalgorithm_V1_coding.txt

clinical_criteria: AKIN Stage 1: ≥ 1.5- to 2-fold increase in serum creatinine from baseline
AKIN Stage 2: 2- to 3-fold increase in serum creatinine from baseline
AKIN Stage 3: > 3-fold increase in serum creatinine from baseline
AKI Block definition: consecutive days with at least one SCr higher than 50% baseline
Transient AKI (tAKI): lasting less than 48 hours
Sustained AKI (sAKI): lasting more than 48 hours
AKI Recurrence: SCr >50% baseline more than 2 days after resolution of previous AKI event

Baseline SCr calculation priority: 1st Line - Median SCr in 7-365 days before presentation; 2nd Line - Minimum SCr between 0-7 days before presentation; 3rd Line - Minimum SCr from presentation to SCr under consideration
algorithm_summary: Rule-based AKI phenotype algorithm that follows KDIGO classification using serum creatinine changes. First excludes patients with pre-existing ESRD (kidney transplant or dialysis codes). Defines baseline SCr using hierarchical approach, then identifies AKI as ≥50% increase from baseline. Stages AKI severity using AKIN classification (1.5x, 2x, >3x increases) and subtypes as transient (<48h) vs sustained (>48h). Algorithm can detect AKI recurrence and multiple AKI blocks within hospitalization. Implementation provided for OMOP v5 database with SQL templates.
